name: New Jersey Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Advisory Committee
provider.parentOrganization: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs

address.description:
State Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
P.O. Box 45002
Newark, New Jersey 07101

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a New Jersey SLP license cost?
Is New Jersey part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does New Jersey allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with a valid NJ license.

mainEntity.name: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in New Jersey. To work in New Jersey, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to New Jersey?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
